# DeathPush - Full Documentation

> A Git GUI for terminal power users. Review diffs, stage changes, and manage branches without opening an IDE. Built for Claude Code, Codex, and Vim workflows.

## About

DeathPush is an open-source Git GUI built with Swift and Rust for macOS. It gives terminal-first developers (Claude Code, Codex, Vim) a fast way to review diffs, stage changes, and manage branches without loading a full IDE.

## Features

### Visual Staging & Diffs
- Visual staging interface for reviewing what changed without opening an IDE
- Inline diff toggles for quick review of changes
- File-level staging and unstaging with single click
- Chunk-level (hunk) staging for granular control over what gets committed
- Commit message editor with conventional commit support

### Monaco-Powered Diff Viewer
- Full Monaco editor integration for viewing diffs
- Syntax highlighting for all major languages
- Side-by-side (split) and unified diff views
- Inline editing capability directly in the diff view
- Word-level diff highlighting for precise change tracking

### Branch Management
- Create new branches from any ref
- Switch between branches
- Merge branches with conflict resolution
- Rebase support (standard and interactive)
- Rename and delete branches
- Full remote tracking with push and pull
- Upstream branch configuration

### Image Diffs
- Visual comparison for image file changes
- Side-by-side comparison mode
- Overlay comparison mode
- Difference highlighting mode
- Supports PNG, JPG, SVG, WebP, and more

### Git Blame & File History
- Line-by-line blame annotations
- Author information and timestamps for each line
- Full file history timeline
- Diff previews for each historical commit
- Navigate through file revisions

### Integrated Terminal
- Built-in terminal with full PTY support
- Run git commands directly
- Execute scripts and shell commands
- Multiple terminal instances
- Terminal persists across view changes

### Advanced Git Operations
- Full stash management (save, pop, apply, drop, list)
- Tag creation and listing (annotated and lightweight)
- Cherry-pick commits between branches
- Reset support (soft, mixed, hard)
- Clean untracked files

### Multi-Window Support
- Open multiple repositories in separate windows
- Each workspace has independent terminal, branches, and state
- Quick switch between recent repositories
- Deep link support for opening repositories from external tools

### Theming
- Multiple built-in themes
- Dark, light, and high-contrast variants
- Customizable workflow aesthetics
- Theme-aware diff highlighting

## Installation

### macOS
1. Download the latest `.dmg` file from the [releases page](https://github.com/felipefdl/deathpush/releases/latest)
2. Open the `.dmg` file
3. Drag DeathPush to your Applications folder
4. Launch DeathPush from Applications
- Supports both Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3/M4) and Intel Macs


## Build from Source

### Prerequisites
- Xcode 16 or later
- Rust 1.85.0 or later
- Git

### Steps
```bash
git clone https://github.com/felipefdl/deathpush.git
cd deathpush
cargo build --release
```

Open `DeathPush/DeathPush.xcodeproj` in Xcode and build.

## Architecture

DeathPush uses a layered architecture:

- **UI**: Native SwiftUI with Monaco editor integration via WKWebView
- **Core**: Rust library linked via FFI
- **Git Operations**: Native git2 library bindings via Rust
- **Terminal**: PTY-based terminal emulation
- **IPC**: Swift-Rust FFI bridge

### Key Design Decisions
- Native Swift UI for macOS-native look and feel
- Rust core for performance-critical git operations
- Monaco editor for VS Code-quality diff viewing
- PTY-based terminal for full shell compatibility

## Supported Git Operations

- `init`, `clone`
- `add`, `reset` (staging/unstaging)
- `commit`, `amend`
- `push`, `pull`, `fetch`
- `branch` (create, delete, rename, list)
- `checkout`, `switch`
- `merge`, `rebase`
- `stash` (save, pop, apply, drop, list)
- `tag` (create, delete, list)
- `cherry-pick`
- `blame`
- `log` (file history, commit history)
- `diff` (staged, unstaged, between commits)
- `remote` (add, remove, list)
- `clean`

## Technical Details

- Stack: Swift (UI), Rust (core)
- License: Apache-2.0
- Platforms: macOS (Apple Silicon & Intel)
- Version: 0.1.0
- Author: Felipe Lima
